steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must connect two sentences: a space agency crashed a probe into Dimorphos, then Dimorphos’s orbital period was permanently altered. The second sentence is the result of the first, so the transition must signal cause and effect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In comparison,’: signals a contrast or comparison between two things — but the two sentences do not compare anything; one causes the other. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In addition,’: signals adding a new, independent piece of information, not a consequence of what came before. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Consequently,’: means “as a result,” directly marking the orbital period change as the outcome of the probe impact. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: signals that what follows narrows or specifies what came before — but the orbital change is an effect, not a specification of the crash. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
